expectations.text: Uses the emitter pattern: a node has a back-edge (downstream step routes back to the emitter), creating a cycle in the Mermaid graph
Cyclic entry node is marked with stadium shape (e.g., `emit([...])`) because auto-detection fails on cycles
expectations.evidence: Only `fetch([Fetch open PRs])` has stadium shape; `emit` itself (the cyclic loop node receiving the back-edge) does NOT. Works because fetch is declared as start, but this is off-pattern vs the skill's guidance: 'Once any node carries stadium shape, those nodes are the start set.' The emitter node is the one typically marked.

expectations.text: Emitter step maintains a cursor in `LOCAL` across re-entries (reads `$LOCAL` / writes `LOCAL: {...}`)
Emitter publishes the current PR item to `GLOBAL` so downstream steps read it generically (e.g., `{{ GLOBAL.item.* }}`)
expectations.evidence: Line 76: `GLOBAL: $(jo pr="$PR")` (uses `pr` instead of `item`, but publishes)

expectations.text: An agent step (plain prose) performs the review summary and references `GLOBAL.*` via Liquid
Has a `done` / terminal branch out of the emitter so the loop can exit (e.g., `emit -->|done| ...`)
expectations.evidence: Line 28: `emit -->|done| done`; emit emits `RESULT: $(jo edge=done)` when exhausted

expectations.text: Every node in the mermaid flow has a matching `## <id>` heading and vice versa
expectations.evidence: fetch, emit, summarize, print, done all present in both flow and steps
